html:
Thanks!

> What is meant by a ‘researcher’s institutional cluster’?

Their institution's compute, if it exists (e.g. an on-prem HPC, lab server, AWS account, etc)

> Especially interested about the wetlab & CRO tie-in

I find three areas particularly interesting right now:

1) Low-cost autonomous experimental wet lab validation. Many folks are working on this one!

2) Cloud labs – like a large core lab with a broad scope – actually make sense now, because lab techs can perform more diverse tasks with uplift from AI. This is most true for assays that are straightforward but previously required specialist training.

3) In some cases, an agent already has the capability to orchestrate CROs, CDMOs, consult human experts (including an IRB), and get to an IND for a rare disease. Costs aside, if you optimize for iteration speed, you could find repurposings incredibly quickly or a get novel investigator initiated IND to to humans in months. It's still very high risk, especially for something novel, and it has low chance of success – but I'd personally want to try this if I had no other options.
